runbooks.title: Hub-Spoke Link Congestion Mitigation
runbooks.symptoms: Sustained rise in hub-facing link utilization (>85%) with upward jitter trend; precedes loss-based SLA breach on spoke tunnels.
runbooks.root_cause: Aggregate spoke traffic exceeding hub uplink capacity during peak windows; no per-spoke shaping.
recommended_actions: Apply QoS shaping / LLQ on the hub uplink to protect priority classes.
Temporarily re-home one high-volume spoke to the secondary hub.
Raise hub uplink committed rate or enable burst allowance if contracted.

runbooks.title: BGP Session Flap & Reroute Cascade
runbooks.symptoms: Increasing BGP flap frequency on a PE-CE session; rising path churn and latency on rerouted prefixes.
runbooks.root_cause: Unstable PE-CE adjacency (link errors or holdtime expiry) triggering repeated best-path recomputation.
recommended_actions: Enable BGP dampening on the affected neighbor to suppress churn.
Inspect physical/optical errors on the PE-CE link; replace if CRC errors climbing.
Pin critical prefixes via a stable backup path until adjacency stabilizes.

runbooks.title: Intermittent MPLS Underlay Loss
runbooks.symptoms: Intermittent packet loss bursts on an LSP with stable utilization; tunnel keepalive jitter rising.
runbooks.root_cause: Flaky underlay segment (P-router or transport) causing transient LSP path degradation.
recommended_actions: Trigger LSP fast-reroute / switch to backup path.
Run targeted MPLS LSP ping/traceroute to localize the lossy hop.
Open transport ticket for the suspect underlay segment.

runbooks.title: Controller Misconfiguration / Policy Drift
runbooks.symptoms: Traffic shifts and latency change following a controller push; metrics diverge from intended policy baseline.
runbooks.root_cause: SDN controller pushed a policy that diverged from the validated intent (mis-scoped path or class map).
recommended_actions: Diff running policy against the last-known-good intent snapshot.
Roll back the offending controller push.
